title: Code Execution, Computer Use, Sandboxes, Prompt Injection, and Artifacts · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved agent trajectory contains one plausible model, schema, tool, authority, state, loop, memory, concurrency, injection, evaluation, telemetry, deployment, or recovery defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deny-by-default disposable action sandbox and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Classify generated code as untrusted and define supported language, runtime, packages, inputs, outputs, time, memory, process, file, network, and secret boundaries.
Run code in a disposable isolated environment with deny-by-default capabilities, immutable base, bounded resources, no host mounts, and complete destruction.
Mediate files, environment, network destinations, DNS, uploads, downloads, subprocesses, and credentials through typed policy and explicit approvals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Artifact Inspection
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-artifact-inspection-1oxm0t" that uses case 8718 to gate code and computer-use actions through disposable sandboxes, deny-by-default capabilities, injection-resistant previews, approvals, and artifact inspection. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Artifact Inspection.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Artifact Inspection into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Artifact Inspection, keep Artifact Inspection connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-artifact-inspection-1oxm0t
# Competency: agentpy-artifact-inspection.
# Case 8718: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter.
def evidence_agentpy_artifact_inspection_1oxm0t(requested_actions: int = 30, sandboxed_actions: int = 12, denied_actions: int = 12, approval_actions: int = 6, host_escapes: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1oxm0t = "1oxm0t-t0mxo1-8718"
    assert evidence_variant_1oxm0t
    evidence_axes_1oxm0t = (
        "authority1oxm0t",
        "interaction8718",
        "toolt0mxo1",
        "state1oxm0t8718",
        "evaluation87181oxm0t",
        "recoveryt0mxo18718",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1oxm0t)) == 6
    if requested_actions < 1 or sandboxed_actions < 0 or denied_actions < 0:
        return False, requested_actions, "sandbox-action-count"
    if sandboxed_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions != requested_actions:
        return False, sandboxed_actions, "sandbox-reconciliation"
    if approval_actions < 2:
        return False, approval_actions, "computer-use-approval"
    if host_escapes > 0:
        return False, host_escapes, "sandbox-escape"
    assert sandboxed_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions == requested_actions and host_escapes == 0
    return True, sandboxed_actions, "contained-agent-actions"
